system_prompt:
You are a clinical documentation assistant inside a prototype clinical AI system.

Task:
Create a concise patient journey summary for a doctor reviewing a synthetic local HIS record.

Rules:
- Use only the supplied patient record.
- Do not diagnose beyond diagnoses already present in the record.
- Do not recommend treatment, medication changes, triage, or follow-up actions.
- Do not invent missing dates, labs, medications, symptoms, or outcomes.
- Mention that this is dummy/synthetic data only when clinically relevant.
- Write in 1 short paragraph, suitable for a doctor-facing patient overview.
- Focus on chronology, presenting complaint, recorded diagnosis, key labs, medications, and note context.
- Return only valid JSON with this shape:
  {"summary":"one paragraph","claims":[{"sentence":"one sentence from the summary","sources":["patient:P001","encounter:E001"]}]}
- Every claim source must use IDs from the supplied record: patient:{patient_id}, encounter:{encounter_id}, lab:{lab_id}, medication:{medication_id}, note:{note_id}.
summary: Aarav Sharma, a 52-year-old male, presented with increased thirst and fatigue, leading to a suspected diagnosis of Type 2 diabetes mellitus on 2026-01-01. He was started on Metformin 500 mg twice daily and advised lifestyle modifications. Follow-up encounters on 2026-02-01 and 2026-03-01 confirmed the diagnosis of Type 2 diabetes mellitus, with lab results showing elevated HbA1c (8.53% on 2026-02-01 and 8.2% on 2026-03-01) and fasting glucose (175 mg/dL on 2026-02-01 and 168 mg/dL on 2026-03-01). Please note that this is synthetic dummy clinical data for pipeline testing only, and no real patient information is present.

claims.sentence: Aarav Sharma, a 52-year-old male, presented with increased thirst and fatigue, leading to a suspected diagnosis of Type 2 diabetes mellitus on 2026-01-01.
He was started on Metformin 500 mg twice daily and advised lifestyle modifications.

Follow-up encounters on 2026-02-01 and 2026-03-01 confirmed the diagnosis of Type 2 diabetes mellitus, with lab results showing elevated HbA1c (8.53% on 2026-02-01 and 8.2% on 2026-03-01) and fasting glucose (175 mg/dL on 2026-02-01 and 168 mg/dL on 2026-03-01).
Please note that this is synthetic dummy clinical data for pipeline testing only, and no real patient information is present.

timeline.detail: Subsequent review for Type 2 diabetes mellitus. Patient journey includes prior evaluation, follow-up, medication review, and interval clinical monitoring. This is synthetic dummy clinical data for pipeline testing only. No real patient information is present.
